is_p_falciparum.evidence: This is a review about the Duffy blood group system and its role as a receptor for Plasmodium vivax. It discusses P. falciparum invasion biology only in passing, not as a study of P. falciparum drug-resistance markers.

reports_target_markers.evidence: No P. falciparum drug-resistance codons (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, Pfcytb) are reported. The paper concerns human FY/DARC gene mutations (e.g. -33T>C, C265T).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: No per-locus parasite marker frequencies are given; the paper describes human blood group genetics, not malaria parasite allele frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: No sub-national frequency data for any parasite marker are provided.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No collection windows for parasite marker frequencies are reported.

african_field_samples.evidence: This is a review; it cites studies from Brazil, Papua New Guinea, etc., but reports no African field parasite isolates of its own.

is_primary_study.evidence: The article is a narrative review ('Very important progress has been made over the last years in understanding the Duffy blood group system'), not a primary empirical study.

supplement.evidence: No relevant per-locus parasite frequency data would be found in supplements; the paper is a review of Duffy/malaria biology.

assessment.summary: This is a narrative review on the Duffy blood group system (DARC) and its function as a receptor for Plasmodium vivax malaria. It does not report P. falciparum drug-resistance markers, no extractable per-locus frequencies, no African field isolates, and is not a primary study. It fails all eligibility criteria.
exclusion_reasons: Not a study of P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (focuses on human Duffy/DARC and P. vivax)
No curated target drug-resistance codons reported
Review article, not a primary empirical study
